top_categories.workout_context: 348 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Around Austria's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
At Race Around Austria, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
21,300ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
61ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
21,300ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race Around Austria's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 348 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 348 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
348 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 21,300ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Around Austria. Train it until it feels boring.
Low-cadence force work for 21,300ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 348 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 348 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Race Around Austria. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Race Around Austria requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and Over Under to prepare you for 348 miles of mixed terrain in St. Georgen im Attergau, Austria.
